description: Multi-turn: after the assistant scopes the first goal, the user corrects the destination ('actually, log it to a Google Sheet instead of notifying me'). The assistant must adapt to the corrected goal in the second turn — not keep pushing the original notification framing, and not restart discovery from scratch. Pins that mid-conversation corrections are honored.
userTurns: I want to get notified when someone fills out my contact form
actually — don't notify me. Just log every submission into a Google Sheet instead.

judge.rubric: In the second turn the user corrected the goal: not a notification, but logging each contact-form submission into a Google Sheet. PASS when the assistant's final reply adapts to that correction — it commits to (or scopes) logging submissions into a Google Sheet and drops the notification as the outcome. Briefly acknowledging the change is fine. FAIL when the assistant ignores the correction and keeps steering toward notifying the user, treats the two goals as unrelated, or throws away all prior context and restarts discovery as if the first turn never happened.
